file_analyses.file_purpose: Core utility module providing comprehensive helper functions for the AURA test automation framework. Contains utilities for file operations, Excel manipulation, Playwright element interactions, screenshot capture, image comparison, flow execution, data loading, animation extraction (SVG/GIF), timestamp generation, and various framework-wide helper functions used across page objects and test flows.

imports.import_statement: from typing import Callable, Optional, Dict
from core.settings import Constants, Config, framework_logger

classes.class_purpose: Custom dictionary subclass that allows attribute-style access to dictionary keys. Enables accessing dictionary values using dot notation (e.g., elements.username_input) instead of bracket notation (e.g., elements['username_input']). Raises AttributeError if a key does not exist when accessed as an attribute.

functions.what_it_does: Generates and returns the current timestamp as a formatted string in the pattern YYYY_MM_DD_HH_MM_SS. Uses datetime.now() to get the current date and time, then applies strftime formatting with underscores as separators. This timestamp format is commonly used for creating unique filenames, directory names, and run identifiers throughout the framework.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The filename string to sanitize by removing invalid characters

functions.what_it_does: Sanitizes a filename string by replacing all invalid filesystem characters with underscores. Uses a regular expression pattern to match and replace characters that are not allowed in filenames on Windows and other operating systems: backslash, forward slash, asterisk, question mark, colon, double quote, less than, greater than, and pipe. Returns the cleaned filename string safe for use in file operations.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: The length of the random string to generate. Defaults to 6 characters.

functions.what_it_does: Generates a random alphanumeric string of specified length using lowercase letters and digits. Creates a character pool by combining string.ascii_lowercase (a-z) and string.digits (0-9), then uses random.choices() to randomly select characters from this pool k times where k equals the length parameter. Joins the selected characters into a single string and returns it. Useful for generating unique identifiers, test data, or temporary names.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text or bytes to hash. Can be a string or bytes object.

functions.what_it_does: Generates a SHA-256 hash of the provided text and returns the first 12 characters of the hexadecimal digest. If the input is already bytes, uses it directly; otherwise converts the text to UTF-8 encoded bytes (treating None as empty string). Computes the SHA-256 hash using hashlib.sha256(), converts to hexadecimal string with hexdigest(), and slices the first 12 characters. This shortened hash is used throughout the framework for creating unique but manageable identifiers for screenshots, files, and cached data.

parameters.name: dirs
parameters.description: Variable number of directory path strings to create

functions.what_it_does: Creates multiple directories if they do not already exist. Accepts a variable number of directory path arguments using *dirs. Iterates through each provided directory path and calls os.makedirs() with exist_ok=True, which creates the directory and any necessary parent directories without raising an error if the directory already exists. This is a convenience function for ensuring output directories exist before writing files.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The attribute name being accessed

functions.what_it_does: Magic method that enables attribute-style access to dictionary keys in the ElementsDict class. When an attribute is accessed using dot notation (e.g., obj.key_name), this method is called. It attempts to return the value associated with the key from the dictionary using self[name]. If the key does not exist, catches the KeyError and raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message indicating that the attribute does not exist on the ElementsDict object.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The attribute name being set

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to assign to the attribute

functions.what_it_does: Magic method that enables setting dictionary keys using attribute-style assignment in the ElementsDict class. When an attribute is assigned using dot notation (e.g., obj.key_name = value), this method is called. It stores the value in the dictionary using self[name] = value, effectively treating attribute assignment as dictionary key assignment. This allows the ElementsDict to be used with both dictionary and object syntax interchangeably.

functions.method_signature: def get_or_create_sheet(workbook, sheet_name: str, headers: list = None)

parameters.name: workbook
parameters.description: The openpyxl Workbook object to operate on

parameters.name: sheet_name
parameters.description: The name of the sheet to retrieve or create

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description: Optional list of header strings to write to the first row. If None, uses Constants.SCRAPE_HEADERS.

functions.docstring: Return the sheet by name, or create it (with headers) if missing.
functions.what_it_does: Retrieves an existing Excel worksheet by name from the workbook, or creates a new one if it does not exist. First checks if sheet_name is in workbook.sheetnames. If found, returns the existing sheet. If not found, creates a new sheet using workbook.create_sheet() with the specified title. Then determines the headers to use: either the provided headers parameter or Constants.SCRAPE_HEADERS as default. Iterates through the headers list and writes each header to the first row of the sheet, starting at column 1. Sets horizontal center alignment for each header cell. Returns the sheet object.

parameters.name: sheet
parameters.description: The openpyxl worksheet to search

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: The selector value to search for in the first column

functions.docstring: Return True if any value in the first column matches 'selector'.
functions.what_it_does: Searches an Excel worksheet to determine if a specific selector value already exists in the first column. Iterates through all rows starting from row 2 (skipping the header row) using sheet.iter_rows() with min_row=2, max_col=1, and values_only=True to get only the values from the first column. For each cell value, checks if it equals the provided selector string. Returns True immediately if a match is found. Returns False if no match is found after checking all rows. This is used to prevent duplicate entries when appending scraped data.
functions.usage_example: exists = find_row(sheet, '[data-testid="login-btn"]')  # Returns True or False

parameters.name: sheet
parameters.description: The openpyxl worksheet to append data to

parameters.name: row_data
parameters.description: List of values representing a row to append to the sheet

functions.docstring: Append row_data to the sheet if not already present (by selector).
functions.what_it_does: Appends a row of scraped data to an Excel worksheet only if the selector (first column value) does not already exist in the sheet. Extracts the selector value from row_data[0]. First checks if the selector is empty or if find_row() returns True indicating the selector already exists. If either condition is true, logs a debug message indicating the row is being skipped as duplicate or empty and returns without appending. If the selector is unique and not empty, appends the entire row_data list to the sheet using sheet.append(). This prevents duplicate scraping of the same UI element.

parameters.name: sheet
parameters.description: The openpyxl worksheet to adjust column widths for

functions.docstring: Resize columns based on maximum content length for readability.
functions.what_it_does: Automatically adjusts the width of all columns in an Excel worksheet based on the maximum content length in each column for improved readability. Iterates through each column in the sheet using sheet.columns. For each column, gets the column letter using get_column_letter() from the first cell's column index. Iterates through all cells in the column to find the maximum length by converting each cell value to string and measuring its length. Sets the column width to the maximum length plus 2 characters for padding using sheet.column_dimensions[col_letter].width. This ensures all content is visible without truncation.

parameters.name: workbook
parameters.description: The openpyxl Workbook object to search

parameters.name: sheet_name
parameters.description: The name of the sheet to retrieve

functions.docstring: Return sheet by name or None (with a warning).
functions.what_it_does: Retrieves an Excel worksheet by name from a workbook, returning None with a warning if not found. First checks if the workbook exists and if sheet_name is in workbook.sheetnames. If both conditions are true, returns the sheet object using workbook[sheet_name]. If the sheet is not found, logs a warning message using framework_logger.warning() indicating the sheet name was not found in the workbook, then returns None. This provides safe sheet access with logging for debugging.
functions.usage_example: sheet = get_sheet_by_name(wb, 'LoginPage')  # Returns sheet or None

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path to the Excel workbook

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description: List of header strings (not used in current implementation)

functions.what_it_does: Loads an existing Excel workbook from the specified path or creates a new empty workbook if the file does not exist. First checks if the file exists at the given path using os.path.exists(). If the file exists, loads it using openpyxl.load_workbook() and gets the active worksheet. If the file does not exist, creates a new Workbook object, removes the default sheet that is automatically created, and sets ws to None. Returns a tuple containing the workbook object and the worksheet object (or None). Note that the headers parameter is accepted but not currently used in the implementation.

parameters.name: wb
parameters.description: The openpyxl Workbook object to save

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path where the workbook should be saved

functions.what_it_does: Saves an Excel workbook to the specified file path, creating the directory structure if it does not exist. First extracts the directory path from the full file path using os.path.dirname(). If the directory path is not empty, creates all necessary parent directories using os.makedirs() with exist_ok=True to avoid errors if directories already exist. Then saves the workbook to the specified path using wb.save(). This ensures the workbook can be saved even if the output directory structure does not yet exist.

parameters.name: excel_path
parameters.description: Path to the Excel file to read contexts from

functions.what_it_does: Extracts and returns a sorted list of unique context names from an Excel file. Loads the workbook from excel_path using openpyxl.load_workbook() and gets the active worksheet. Creates an empty set called contexts to store unique context values. Iterates through all rows starting from row 2 (skipping header) using ws.iter_rows() with min_row=2 and values_only=True. For each row, unpacks the first two values as flow and context. If both flow and context are not empty, adds the context to the set. After processing all rows, converts the set to a sorted list and returns it. This is used to discover all unique test contexts defined in test data files.

parameters.name: elem
parameters.description: Playwright ElementHandle to check visibility and enabled state

functions.what_it_does: Checks if a Playwright element is hidden or disabled by evaluating JavaScript in the browser context. Uses elem.evaluate() to execute JavaScript that checks two conditions: whether el.offsetParent is null (indicating the element is hidden via CSS display:none or visibility:hidden) or whether el.disabled is true (indicating a form element is disabled). Returns True if either condition is met, False otherwise. This is more reliable than simple visibility checks as it detects elements hidden by CSS properties.

parameters.name: elem
parameters.description: Playwright ElementHandle to get position information from

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the position and dimensions of a Playwright element as a dictionary. Calls elem.bounding_box() to get the element's bounding box information, which returns a dictionary with x, y, width, and height keys, or None if the element is not visible. Uses the or operator with an empty dict as fallback if bounding_box returns None. Constructs and returns a new dictionary with integer values for x, y, width, and height coordinates, using box.get() with default value 0 for each property and converting to int. This provides consistent position data even for hidden elements.

parameters.name: elem
parameters.description: Playwright ElementHandle to extract computed styles from

functions.what_it_does: Extracts computed CSS styles from a Playwright element by executing JavaScript in the browser context. Uses elem.evaluate() to run a JavaScript function that gets the computed styles using window.getComputedStyle(el). Constructs and returns a dictionary object containing six style properties: font_family, font_size, font_weight, color, background (backgroundColor), and text_align. Each property uses the logical OR operator to provide an empty string as fallback if the style property is undefined. This is used during scraping to capture visual styling information for validation purposes.

parameters.name: elem
parameters.description: Playwright ElementHandle to extract text from

functions.what_it_does: Extracts the visible inner text from a Playwright element and returns it as a stripped string. Calls elem.inner_text() to get the text content of the element as it appears to the user (excluding hidden elements and respecting CSS display properties). Then calls strip() on the result to remove leading and trailing whitespace. Returns the cleaned text string. This is the primary method for extracting text content during scraping operations.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object to freeze scrolling on

functions.what_it_does: Disables scrolling on a web page by injecting CSS that sets overflow to hidden on html and body elements. Uses page.add_style_tag() to inject a style tag with content 'html, body { overflow: hidden !important; }' into the page. The !important flag ensures this style overrides any existing overflow styles. This is useful when capturing screenshots or performing visual comparisons where you want to prevent the page from scrolling during the operation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object to restore scrolling on

functions.what_it_does: Re-enables scrolling on a web page by injecting CSS that sets overflow to auto on html and body elements. Uses page.add_style_tag() to inject a style tag with content 'html, body { overflow: auto !important; }' into the page. The !important flag ensures this style overrides the previous freeze_scroll style. This restores normal scrolling behavior after it was disabled by freeze_scroll(). Should be called after screenshot or visual comparison operations are complete.

functions.method_signature: def take_element_screenshot(page: Page, selector: str, output_path: str) -> bool

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object containing the element

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: CSS selector or other locator string to identify the element

parameters.name: output_path
parameters.description: File path where the screenshot should be saved

functions.what_it_does: Captures a screenshot of a specific element on the page identified by a selector string. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. First locates the element using page.locator(selector). Checks if the element count is 0 or if the element is not visible using is_visible(), returning False if either condition is true. Extracts the directory path from output_path and creates it if it does not exist using os.makedirs() with exist_ok=True. Calls element.screenshot() with the output path to capture and save the screenshot. Returns True if successful. If any exception occurs, logs a warning with the selector and error message using framework_logger.warning() and returns False.

functions.method_signature: def take_element_handle_screenshot(handle: ElementHandle, output_path: str) -> bool

parameters.name: handle
parameters.description: Playwright ElementHandle object to capture screenshot of

parameters.name: output_path
parameters.description: File path where the screenshot should be saved

functions.what_it_does: Captures a screenshot of a specific element using an ElementHandle object. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. First checks if the element is visible using handle.is_visible(), returning False if not visible. Extracts the directory path from output_path and creates it if it does not exist using os.makedirs() with exist_ok=True. Calls handle.screenshot() with the output path to capture and save the screenshot. Returns True if successful. If any exception occurs, logs a warning with the error message using framework_logger.warning() and returns False. This is similar to take_element_screenshot but works with ElementHandle objects instead of selector strings.

functions.method_signature: def save_full_page_screenshot(page: Page, flow_name: str, context_name: str, output_dir: str = None) -> str

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object to capture screenshot of

parameters.name: flow_name
parameters.description: Name of the test flow for filename generation

parameters.name: context_name
parameters.description: Name of the context/checkpoint for filename generation

parameters.name: output_dir
parameters.description: Optional output directory path. If None, uses Constants.VALIDATE_OUTPUT_DIR.

functions.what_it_does: Captures a full-page screenshot and saves it with a timestamped filename. Determines the output directory using the provided output_dir parameter or Constants.VALIDATE_OUTPUT_DIR as default. Creates the directory if it does not exist using os.makedirs() with exist_ok=True. Generates a filename by combining flow_name, context_name, and current timestamp from get_current_timestamp(), then sanitizes it using sanitize_filename() and appends .png extension. Constructs the full output path by joining the directory and filename. Calls page.screenshot() with the path and full_page=True to capture the entire scrollable page. Returns the filename string (not the full path). This is used during validation to capture baseline and comparison screenshots.

functions.method_signature: def highlight_validation(ws, row_cells, passed: bool = None)

parameters.name: ws
parameters.description: The openpyxl worksheet containing the validation results

parameters.name: row_cells
parameters.description: List of cell objects representing a row to highlight

parameters.name: passed
parameters.description: Optional boolean indicating pass/fail status (not currently used in logic)

functions.what_it_does: Applies color highlighting to validation result rows in Excel based on Text_Result and Screenshot_Result column values. First extracts the header row from the worksheet and creates a dictionary mapping column names to their indices. Defines a helper function norm() that normalizes cell values to uppercase stripped strings. Gets the normalized values of Text_Result and Screenshot_Result columns from the row. If both are empty or N/A, returns without highlighting. Then applies complex conditional logic to determine the appropriate fill color from Constants: PASS (both pass), PASS_TEXT_ONLY (text pass, screenshot N/A), PASS_WITH_EXCLUDES (pass with exclusions), or FAIL (any failure). Finally applies the determined fill color to all cells in the row. This provides visual feedback in validation reports.

functions.method_signature: def generate_report(data: list, output_dir: str, filename: str = "validation_results")

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: List of dictionaries containing validation result data

parameters.name: output_dir
parameters.description: Directory path where report files should be saved

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Base filename for the report files without extension. Defaults to 'validation_results'.

functions.what_it_does: Generates both JSON and HTML validation reports from validation result data. Creates the output directory if it does not exist using os.makedirs(). Constructs file paths for JSON and HTML reports by joining output_dir with filename and appropriate extensions. Writes the data list as formatted JSON to the JSON file using json.dump() with indent=2. Then creates an HTML report by writing HTML structure with a table. If data is not empty, extracts headers from the first dictionary's keys and writes them as table headers. Iterates through each row dictionary and writes table rows with values for each header. Closes the HTML tags. This provides human-readable and machine-parseable validation reports.

functions.method_signature: def write_excel_context(rows, wb, sheet_name, path, header, highlight_func=None)

parameters.name: rows
parameters.description: List of row data to write (can be dicts or lists)

parameters.name: wb
parameters.description: Openpyxl Workbook object

parameters.name: sheet_name
parameters.description: Name of the sheet to write to

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: File path to save the workbook

parameters.name: header
parameters.description: List of header column names

parameters.name: highlight_func
parameters.description: Optional function to call for highlighting each row after writing

functions.what_it_does: Writes validation or scraping data rows to an Excel sheet with header management and optional row highlighting. Gets or creates the sheet by name. If sheet is empty, writes the header row and applies header fill color from Constants.COLOR_HEADER. If sheet has data, validates the first row matches the expected header and replaces it if different. Identifies and deletes empty rows and duplicate header rows. Iterates through the provided rows, skipping empty rows. For each row, if it's a dictionary, extracts values in header order; if it's a list, uses it directly. Appends the row to the sheet. If highlight_func is provided, calls it with the worksheet, the newly added row cells, and None. Finally saves the workbook to the specified path. This is the core function for writing test results to Excel.

parameters.name: results
parameters.description: List of validation result dictionaries

parameters.name: wb
parameters.description: Openpyxl Workbook object

parameters.name: sheet_name
parameters.description: Name of the sheet to write to

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: File path to save the workbook

functions.what_it_does: Convenience wrapper function for writing validation report data to Excel with automatic highlighting. Calls write_excel_context() with the provided results, workbook, sheet_name, and path parameters. Uses Constants.VALIDATION_HEADERS as the header list and highlight_validation as the highlight_func parameter. This ensures validation reports are written with the correct header structure and automatic pass/fail color highlighting based on validation results. Simplifies the common use case of writing validation reports.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: CamelCase string to convert to snake_case

functions.docstring:
Convert CamelCase flow names to snake_case module/function names.
e.g. CreateV3Account -> create_v3_account
functions.what_it_does: Converts CamelCase strings to snake_case format using regular expressions. First applies re.sub() with pattern '(.)([A-Z][a-z]+)' and replacement r'\1_\2' to insert underscores before uppercase letters followed by lowercase letters. Then applies another re.sub() with pattern '([a-z0-9])([A-Z])' and replacement r'\1_\2' to handle transitions from lowercase/digits to uppercase. Finally converts the entire result to lowercase using lower(). This is used to convert flow class names like 'CreateV3Account' to module/function names like 'create_v3_account' for dynamic imports.

parameters.name: flow_args
parameters.description: Dictionary of flow arguments containing language/locale information

functions.what_it_does: Extracts and returns the language code from flow arguments dictionary. Uses dict.get() to retrieve the value associated with the key 'tenant_country_language_code' from the flow_args dictionary. Returns the language code string if present, or None if the key does not exist. This is used throughout the framework to determine which language-specific test data and localization to use during test execution.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Target platform identifier (e.g., 'web_chrome', 'GothamDesktop')

parameters.name: simulator_platform
parameters.description: Simulator platform identifier used when target does not start with 'web_'

functions.docstring: Get the target directory name based on target and simulator_platform parameters from flow args.
functions.what_it_does: Determines the appropriate target directory name based on platform identifiers. Defines a comprehensive target_folder_mapping dictionary that maps various platform identifiers to standardized folder names (e.g., 'web_chrome' to 'chrome', 'GothamDesktop' to 'wingotham', 'Android' to 'android', etc.). Checks if the target parameter starts with 'web_'. If yes, looks up the target in the mapping dictionary and returns the mapped value or the original target if not found. If target does not start with 'web_', looks up simulator_platform in the mapping and returns the mapped value or the original target if not found. This standardizes directory naming across different execution platforms.

functions.method_signature: def run_flow(mode: str, flow_name: str, flow_args: Optional[Dict] = None, context_override: Optional[str] = None) -> None

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Execution mode: 'scrape', 'validate', or 'noop'

parameters.name: flow_name
parameters.description: Name of the test flow to execute

parameters.name: flow_args
parameters.description: Optional dictionary of flow arguments including locale, target, etc.

parameters.name: context_override
parameters.description: Optional context name override (not currently used in implementation)

functions.what_it_does: Core flow execution orchestrator that dynamically loads and runs test flows with scrape, validate, or noop modes. Validates flow_name is provided. Initializes flow_args to empty dict if None. Converts flow_name to snake_case module name using camel_to_snake(). Constructs module path as 'test_flows.{flow_name}.{module_basename}'. Dynamically imports the flow module using importlib.import_module(), catching ModuleNotFoundError and logging errors. Validates the module has a function matching module_basename. Extracts configuration from flow_args including run_id timestamp, lang_code, target platform, and log collection settings. Based on mode, sets up appropriate output directories and defines a stage_callback function that calls either scrape_context or validate_context. Initializes Config with all flow arguments. Optionally starts HTML report generation. Executes the flow function with stage_callback, catching exceptions and marking flow as failed. Finalizes the report. Checks for step failures or interruptions and raises SystemExit(1) if validation failed. Logs completion message with output directory. This is the central flow execution engine.

functions.method_signature: def run_scrape_mode(flow_name: str, flow_args: Dict) -> None

parameters.name: flow_name
parameters.description: Name of the test flow to scrape

parameters.name: flow_args
parameters.description: Dictionary of flow arguments

functions.what_it_does: Convenience wrapper function for executing a test flow in scrape mode. Simply calls run_flow() with mode parameter set to 'scrape', passing through the flow_name and flow_args parameters. This provides a cleaner API for callers who want to explicitly run scrape mode without specifying the mode string.

functions.method_signature: def run_validate_mode(flow_name: str, flow_args: Dict) -> None

parameters.name: flow_name
parameters.description: Name of the test flow to validate

parameters.name: flow_args
parameters.description: Dictionary of flow arguments

functions.what_it_does: Convenience wrapper function for executing a test flow in validate mode. Simply calls run_flow() with mode parameter set to 'validate', passing through the flow_name and flow_args parameters. This provides a cleaner API for callers who want to explicitly run validate mode without specifying the mode string.

functions.method_signature: def run_noop_mode(flow_name: str, flow_args: Dict) -> None

parameters.name: flow_name
parameters.description: Name of the test flow to run in noop mode

parameters.name: flow_args
parameters.description: Dictionary of flow arguments

functions.what_it_does: Convenience wrapper function for executing a test flow in noop mode. Simply calls run_flow() with mode parameter set to 'noop', passing through the flow_name and flow_args parameters. Noop mode executes the flow logic without performing any scraping or validation operations, useful for dry runs and flow structure validation.

functions.method_signature: def load_json_file(file_path: str, encoding: str = "utf-8") -> dict

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the JSON file to load

parameters.name: encoding
parameters.description: Character encoding to use when reading the file. Defaults to 'utf-8'.

functions.what_it_does: Loads and parses a JSON file, returning the parsed data as a dictionary with error handling. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Opens the file at file_path with the specified encoding using open() in read mode. Parses the JSON content using json.load() and returns the resulting dictionary. If FileNotFoundError is raised, re-raises it with a descriptive message including the file path. If json.JSONDecodeError is raised during parsing, re-raises it as a ValueError with a message including the file path and original error. This provides clear error messages for common JSON loading failures.

Loads locale mapping data from a JSON file defined in Constants.LOCALE_MAP_PATH. Simply calls load_json_file() with the path from Constants.LOCALE_MAP_PATH and returns the parsed dictionary. This provides centralized access to locale configuration data used throughout the framework for internationalization and localization.

Loads printer profile configuration data from a JSON file defined in Constants.PRINTER_PROFILES_PATH. Simply calls load_json_file() with the path from Constants.PRINTER_PROFILES_PATH and returns the parsed dictionary. This provides centralized access to printer profile data including SKUs, regions, and capabilities used for test data generation.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to retrieve address data for

functions.what_it_does: Loads address data for a specific country from the address data JSON file. First calls load_json_file() with Constants.ADDRESS_DATA_PATH to load the complete address data dictionary. Then uses dict.get() to retrieve and return the address data for the specified country key. Returns None if the country is not found in the data. This provides test data for address fields during enrollment and checkout flows.

functions.method_signature: def get_printer_details_for_country(profile_name: str, region: str, printer_profiles: dict, easy_enroll_flag: str = None, biz_model: str = "Flex") -> tuple

parameters.name: profile_name
parameters.description: Name of the printer profile to retrieve

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Region identifier to get SKUs for

parameters.name: printer_profiles
parameters.description: Dictionary of printer profile data loaded from JSON

parameters.name: easy_enroll_flag
parameters.description: Optional 'yes' or 'no' string to filter SKUs by easy_enroll capability

parameters.name: biz_model
parameters.description: Business model identifier. Defaults to 'Flex'.

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves printer details including SKU and supply variant for a specific profile, region, and optional easy enroll filter. First validates that profile_name exists in printer_profiles dictionary, raising ValueError if not found. Gets the SKUs dictionary for the profile and validates that region exists, raising ValueError if not found. Retrieves the list of SKUs for the region. If easy_enroll_flag is provided, converts it to boolean and filters the SKUs list to only include SKUs where the easy_enroll property matches the desired value, raising ValueError if no matching SKUs are found. Selects the first SKU from the filtered or unfiltered list. Extracts the sku and supplyvariant values from the selected SKU dictionary. Returns a tuple containing (profile_name, sku, supplyvariant, biz_model). This is used to generate printer configuration for test flows.

parameters.name: svg_xml
parameters.description: SVG XML content as string to extract animation duration from

parameters.name: fallback_duration_ms
parameters.description: Fallback duration in milliseconds if no duration is found. Defaults to 1000ms.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts animation duration from SVG XML content by searching for SMIL or CSS animation duration attributes. First uses re.search() to look for SMIL dur attribute with pattern 'dur="([\d\.]+)(ms|s)"'. If found, extracts the numeric value and unit. Converts to milliseconds if unit is 'ms', or multiplies by 1000 if unit is 's', then returns as integer. If SMIL duration not found, searches for CSS animation-duration property with pattern 'animation-duration:\s*([\d\.]+)(ms|s)'. If found, performs the same conversion and returns. If neither pattern is found, returns the fallback_duration_ms value. This is used to determine appropriate frame capture timing for animated SVGs.

functions.method_signature: def extract_animated_svgs(page, min_size=5120, poll_interval=0.02, max_frames=100, upscale_factor=4, save_debug_frames=True, debug_frame_dir="./debug_svg_frames")

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object to extract SVGs from

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum SVG size in bytes to consider. Defaults to 5120.

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Time in seconds between frame captures. Defaults to 0.02s (50fps).

parameters.name: max_frames
parameters.description: Maximum number of frames to capture. Defaults to 100.

parameters.name: upscale_factor
parameters.description: Factor to upscale SVG dimensions for higher quality capture. Defaults to 4.

parameters.name: save_debug_frames
parameters.description: Whether to save individual debug frames. Defaults to True.

parameters.name: debug_frame_dir
parameters.description: Directory to save debug frames. Defaults to './debug_svg_frames'.

functions.what_it_does: Comprehensive function that detects, captures, and converts animated SVG elements on a page to GIF format with extensive animation detection logic. Queries all SVG elements on the page. For each SVG: checks visibility, extracts outer HTML, validates size against min_size threshold. Detects animation using multiple strategies: SMIL tags (animate, animateTransform), CSS animations and transitions via computed styles, JavaScript timers and requestAnimationFrame, script tags and event handlers. Logs detailed debug information about animation detection. Performs hard reset of animations by removing and re-adding elements to DOM. Triggers animations via hover and mouseenter events. Waits fixed initial delay. Captures multiple detect frames to identify pixel-level changes using PIL Image comparison and hash comparison. If animation detected, upscales SVG dimensions by upscale_factor. Captures frames at poll_interval timing, detecting loop points by comparing frame hashes to first frame. Calculates per-frame duration based on detected loop period. Restores original SVG dimensions. Converts captured frames to GIF using PIL with optimized palette, calculated duration, and disposal mode 2. Returns list of result dictionaries containing element handle, SVG XML, GIF bytes, frame count, resolution, animation type, and timing metadata. This is the most complex animation extraction function in the framework.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object to extract GIFs from

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum GIF file size in bytes to consider. Defaults to 1024.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts animated GIF images from a web page by finding img elements with .gif sources and downloading them. Queries all img elements with src attributes containing '.gif' using page.query_selector_all(). For each GIF element: checks if visible, gets the src attribute, validates it contains '.gif'. Makes an HTTP GET request to the GIF URL using page.context.request.get(). Checks response status is 200. Gets the response body as bytes. Validates the size is at least min_size bytes. Opens the GIF bytes with PIL Image and checks if it has is_animated attribute set to True and n_frames > 1 to confirm it's actually animated. If all checks pass, appends a result dictionary containing the element handle, src URL, bytes content, and size to the results list. Returns the list of extracted animated GIFs. Catches and logs exceptions for individual GIFs without stopping the entire extraction.

functions.method_signature: def extract_animated_visuals(page, selector=".stepsImage", min_size=1024, screenshot_dir=".", context_hint=None, poll_interval=0.03, max_frames=60, detect_loop=True, save_debug_frames=False)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object to extract animations from

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: CSS selector to find animation containers. Defaults to '.stepsImage'.

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum output file size in bytes. Defaults to 1024.

parameters.name: screenshot_dir
parameters.description: Directory to save output files. Defaults to current directory.

parameters.name: context_hint
parameters.description: Optional context name for filename generation

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Time in seconds between frame captures. Defaults to 0.03s.

parameters.name: max_frames
parameters.description: Maximum frames to capture. Defaults to 60.

parameters.name: detect_loop
parameters.description: Whether to detect animation loops. Defaults to True.

parameters.name: save_debug_frames
parameters.description: Whether to save individual frame PNGs. Defaults to False.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts animated visual elements (SVG, Canvas, Lottie, CSS sprites) from page elements matching a selector and converts them to GIF or PNG. Queries all elements matching the selector. For each element: checks visibility, gets inner HTML and tag name. Detects animation type by checking for SVG tags, canvas elements, 'lottie' in HTML, or CSS background-image sprites. Generates filename using context_hint and animation type. Captures frames in a loop up to max_frames: forces repaint by setting random outline style, scrolls element into view, takes screenshot, converts to PIL Image, computes SHA-256 hash of image bytes, stores frame and hash. Optionally saves debug frame PNGs if save_debug_frames is True. Detects loop by comparing current frame hash to first frame hash after frame 3. Determines if animated by checking for multiple unique frame hashes. If animated with 2+ frames: calculates duration based on detected loop period or poll_interval, converts frames to palette mode, saves as optimized GIF with calculated duration and loop=0. If static: saves first frame as PNG. Validates output size against min_size and deletes if too small. Returns list of result dictionaries with element, animation_type, screenshot_path, is_animated flag, frames_captured count, duration_per_frame_ms, size, and tag. This provides flexible animation extraction for various animation technologies.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the largest and most comprehensive utility module in the AURA framework. It serves as the central repository for all framework-wide helper functions. Key functional areas include: (1) Excel operations with openpyxl for reading/writing test data and validation results with automatic formatting and highlighting, (2) Playwright element interaction utilities for screenshots, style extraction, position detection, and visibility checks, (3) Flow execution orchestration with dynamic module loading and mode-based execution (scrape/validate/noop), (4) Advanced animation extraction supporting SVG (SMIL/CSS), GIF, Canvas, and Lottie with frame capture, loop detection, and GIF generation, (5) File system utilities for directory creation, filename sanitization, and path management, (6) Data loading functions for JSON configuration files including locale, printer profiles, and address data, (7) Image comparison and validation highlighting for visual regression testing, (8) Timestamp and hash generation for unique identifiers. The extract_animated_svgs function is particularly sophisticated with 250+ lines implementing comprehensive animation detection using multiple strategies (SMIL tags, CSS properties, JavaScript timers, pixel diffing) and advanced frame capture with loop detection and timing synchronization. The run_flow function is the core orchestrator that ties together the entire framework execution pipeline. This module has no UI locators as it provides backend utilities rather than page interactions.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Core validation module responsible for comparing live UI against baseline scraped data during validation runs. Performs text validation, screenshot comparison with pixel-level diffing, exclusion region handling, animation validation (SVG/GIF), and generates detailed Excel validation reports with pass/fail highlighting. Supports full-page and element-level validation with suggested exclusion regions for visual differences.

imports.import_statement: from core.utils import sanitize_filename, load_or_create_workbook, save_workbook, highlight_validation, save_full_page_screenshot, take_element_screenshot, take_element_handle_screenshot, hash_text, ensure_dirs, write_report_excel_context, extract_animated_svgs, extract_animated_gifs

parameters.name: exclude_str
parameters.description: String representation of exclusion regions, typically from Excel cell

functions.what_it_does: Parses exclusion region string into a list of tuples representing rectangular areas to exclude from image comparison. Returns empty list if exclude_str is empty or None. Uses ast.literal_eval() to safely parse the string as a Python literal (list or tuple). If the result is a single tuple (x, y, width, height), wraps it in a list. Returns the list of exclusion tuples. If parsing fails due to invalid syntax, catches the exception and returns empty list. This allows validation to ignore specific screen regions that are expected to differ (timestamps, dynamic content, etc.).

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X coordinate of pixel to check

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Y coordinate of pixel to check

parameters.name: exclusions
parameters.description: List of exclusion region tuples (x, y, width, height)

functions.what_it_does: Checks if a pixel coordinate falls within any of the defined exclusion regions. Iterates through each exclusion tuple in the exclusions list. For each exclusion, unpacks the four values as ex_x, ex_y, ex_w, ex_h representing the top-left corner and dimensions of the exclusion rectangle. Checks if the provided x coordinate is within the range [ex_x, ex_x + ex_w) and the y coordinate is within [ex_y, ex_y + ex_h). Returns True immediately if the pixel is inside any exclusion region. Returns False if the pixel is not inside any exclusion region after checking all. This is used during pixel-by-pixel image comparison to skip pixels in excluded areas.

parameters.name: pixels
parameters.description: List of (x, y) tuples representing failed pixel coordinates

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Tuple of (width, height) representing image dimensions

functions.what_it_does: Converts a list of failed pixel coordinates into bounding box rectangles using flood fill algorithm. Returns empty list if no pixels provided. Unpacks width and height from size tuple. Creates a binary mask image with all pixels set to 0. Sets mask pixels to 255 for each failed pixel coordinate. Creates a visited set to track processed pixels. Defines inner flood_fill function that performs iterative flood fill using a stack: starts from a seed pixel, explores all connected pixels with value 255, tracks min/max x and y coordinates, returns bounding box tuple (xmin, ymin, width, height). Iterates through all pixels in the mask. For each unvisited pixel with value 255, calls flood_fill to get its bounding box. Only adds boxes where both width and height are >= MIN_TOLERANCE_PIXELS (1). Returns list of bounding box tuples. This generates suggested exclusion regions for visual differences by grouping adjacent failed pixels into rectangular regions.

parameters.name: img1_path
parameters.description: Path to baseline image file

parameters.name: img2_path
parameters.description: Path to validation image file

parameters.name: exclusions
parameters.description: List of exclusion region tuples

parameters.name: color_tolerance
parameters.description: Maximum allowed color difference per pixel. Defaults to COLOR_TOLERANCE constant (35).

parameters.name: original_exclude_str
parameters.description: Original exclusion string from Excel. If '[]', returns pass immediately.

functions.what_it_does: Performs pixel-by-pixel comparison of two images with exclusion region support and generates suggested exclusion boxes for differences. Returns (False, None, [], []) if either image file does not exist. If original_exclude_str is '[]', returns (True, None, [], []) immediately treating empty exclusion list as pass. Opens both images and converts to RGB mode. Gets dimensions of both images and determines the maximum width and height. Creates two new white base images of the maximum dimensions and pastes the original images at (0,0) to handle size mismatches. Initializes two lists: failed_pixels for all differences and failed_pixels_outside_exclusions for differences not in exclusion regions. Iterates through every pixel coordinate in the combined image dimensions. For each pixel, gets RGB values from both images, calculates Euclidean distance between colors, and if distance exceeds color_tolerance, adds to failed_pixels list and checks if pixel is outside exclusions using is_in_exclusion() - if outside, adds to failed_pixels_outside_exclusions. After checking all pixels, calls get_bounding_boxes() on failed_pixels_outside_exclusions to generate suggested exclusion regions. Returns (True, None, [], failed_pixels) if no boxes generated (pass). Returns (False, None, boxes, failed_pixels_outside_exclusions) if boxes exist (fail). This is the core image comparison engine with smart exclusion handling.

parameters.name: img2_path
parameters.description: Path to validation image to overlay failures on

parameters.name: failed_pixels_outside_exclusions
parameters.description: List of (x, y) tuples for pixels that failed outside exclusions

parameters.name: out_path
parameters.description: Output path for the overlaid diff image

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.description: Alpha transparency value for red overlay (0-255). Defaults to 120.

functions.what_it_does: Creates a visual diff image by overlaying red semi-transparent pixels on failed areas of the validation image. Opens the validation image at img2_path and converts to RGBA mode to support transparency. Creates a new transparent overlay image of the same size with all pixels set to (255, 0, 0, 0) - red with full transparency. Gets the pixel data accessor for the overlay. Unpacks width and height from image size. Iterates through each failed pixel coordinate in failed_pixels_outside_exclusions. For each coordinate, checks if it's within image bounds, and if so, sets the overlay pixel to (255, 0, 0, alpha) - red with specified transparency. Uses Image.alpha_composite() to blend the validation image with the red overlay, creating a composite image where failed pixels appear as semi-transparent red. Saves the composite image to out_path. Returns True on success. This creates intuitive visual diff images showing exactly where validation failed.

functions.method_signature: def compare_gif_files(gif_path_1: str, gif_path_2: str) -> bool

parameters.name: gif_path_1
parameters.description: Path to first GIF file

parameters.name: gif_path_2
parameters.description: Path to second GIF file

functions.docstring: Compare two GIF files byte-wise. Returns True if identical.
functions.what_it_does: Performs byte-by-byte comparison of two GIF files to determine if they are identical. First checks if both files exist using os.path.exists(). If either file is missing, logs a warning with both file paths and returns False. Opens both files in binary read mode using context managers. Reads the entire content of both files using f.read() and compares them with == operator. Returns True if the byte content is identical, False otherwise. This is used for validating animated GIF assets during validation runs.

functions.method_signature: def compare_svg_xml(svg_xml_1: str, svg_xml_2: str) -> bool

parameters.name: svg_xml_1
parameters.description: First SVG XML content as string

parameters.name: svg_xml_2
parameters.description: Second SVG XML content as string

functions.docstring:
Robust SVG comparison: ignores whitespace, attribute order, float precision, and comment nodes.
Handles: attribute sorting, rounds floats, strips comments and normalizes text/tail.
functions.what_it_does: Performs robust semantic comparison of two SVG XML strings with normalization to ignore insignificant differences. Defines inner normalize_float() function that rounds float values to 3 decimals and handles space/comma-separated float lists in path data. Defines inner clean_element() function that recursively processes XML elements: sorts attributes alphabetically, normalizes float values in attributes, strips text and tail whitespace, removes comment nodes, sorts child elements by tag+attributes+text for deterministic order. Defines inner get_root() function that parses XML string using ET.fromstring() with error handling. Parses both SVG strings to get root elements. If either parse fails, falls back to whitespace-insensitive string comparison using re.sub() to remove all whitespace. If both parse successfully, applies clean_element() to both roots to normalize them. Serializes both normalized trees back to strings using ET.tostring(). Compares the normalized strings and returns True if identical, False otherwise. This handles common SVG differences like attribute order, float precision, whitespace, and comments that don't affect visual rendering.

functions.method_signature: def validate_context(flow_name, context_name, page, excel_path, output_folder, validation_workbook_path=None, screenshot_only=False, sub_context=None, lang_code=None, animations=False, target_dir=None)

parameters.name: flow_name
parameters.description: Name of the test flow being validated

parameters.name: context_name
parameters.description: Name of the context/checkpoint being validated

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object of the live page to validate

parameters.name: excel_path
parameters.description: Path to baseline Excel file with scraped data

parameters.name: output_folder
parameters.description: Directory to save validation results

parameters.name: validation_workbook_path
parameters.description: Optional path to validation results workbook. Auto-generated if None.

parameters.name: screenshot_only
parameters.description: If True, only captures full-page screenshot without element validation

parameters.name: sub_context
parameters.description: Optional sub-context name for granular checkpoints

parameters.name: lang_code
parameters.description: Language code for filename generation

parameters.name: animations
parameters.description: If True, validates animated SVG and GIF assets

parameters.name: target_dir
parameters.description: Target platform directory name

functions.what_it_does: Core validation function that compares live page state against baseline scraped data with comprehensive element, text, screenshot, and animation validation. Creates output directories for validation screenshots, failure screenshots, and animations. Constructs subcontext suffix for filenames. If screenshot_only mode: captures full-page screenshot with retry logic, loads baseline Excel to find matching full-page baseline, performs image comparison with exclusions, generates validation report row with pass/fail status and suggested exclusion regions if failed, overlays red highlighting on failures, writes report to Excel, and returns early. For full validation: loads baseline workbook and finds the context sheet, extracts header row, filters baseline data rows matching context and sub_context. If animations flag is True: extracts all animated SVGs from page using extract_animated_svgs() and saves them to validation_animations folder. Queries all visible elements on live page with text content and bounding boxes, storing them in live_elements list. Iterates through each baseline row: handles animation type rows by extracting and comparing GIF or SVG animations byte-wise or semantically, handles fullpage type rows by capturing and comparing full-page screenshots with exclusions, handles regular element rows by finding matching live elements by text and tag, comparing text and href, capturing element screenshots, comparing with baseline screenshots using exclusions, generating suggested exclusion boxes for failures, and overlaying red highlighting on diff images. Builds comprehensive validation report rows with all comparison results. Writes all results to Excel with automatic highlighting using write_report_excel_context(). Logs completion. This is the most complex validation function implementing the entire validation pipeline with support for multiple validation types and detailed reporting.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the core validation engine for the AURA framework. The validate_context() function is the centerpiece with 350+ lines implementing comprehensive validation logic. Key capabilities: (1) Screenshot-only mode for quick full-page captures, (2) Element-level validation matching live elements to baseline by text and tag, (3) Pixel-perfect image comparison with configurable color tolerance (35 by default), (4) Exclusion region support to ignore expected differences like timestamps or dynamic content, (5) Automatic generation of suggested exclusion regions using flood-fill algorithm to group adjacent failed pixels into bounding boxes, (6) Animation validation for both GIF (byte-wise comparison) and SVG (semantic XML comparison with normalization), (7) Visual diff generation with red overlay highlighting exact failure pixels, (8) Comprehensive Excel reporting with automatic color highlighting (green for pass, red for fail, yellow for pass with exclusions), (9) Support for sub-contexts enabling granular validation checkpoints, (10) Retry logic for screenshot capture to handle timing issues. The compare_svg_xml() function is particularly sophisticated, implementing robust semantic comparison that ignores insignificant differences like whitespace, attribute order, float precision, and comments while detecting actual visual changes. The image comparison uses Euclidean distance in RGB color space with configurable tolerance. The module integrates tightly with core.utils for screenshot capture, Excel operations, and animation extraction. No UI locators as this is a backend validation engine.

file_analyses.file_purpose: HTML documentation file providing comprehensive CLI help and usage instructions for the AURA test automation framework. Documents all command-line parameters, execution modes (scrape/validate/noop), context and subcontext concepts, screenshot-only mode, supported platforms, and includes detailed usage examples for various test scenarios.
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a static HTML documentation file that serves as the user manual for the AURA CLI. It contains no executable code, classes, methods, or locators. The file provides comprehensive documentation including: (1) Table of contents with anchor links to all sections, (2) Usage syntax showing the command structure with all parameters, (3) Detailed parameter descriptions for --flow, --mode, --stack, --locale, --language, --printer_profile, --easy_enroll, --biz_model, --headless, --screenshot_only, --target, and --debug, (4) Explanation of three execution modes: scrape (capture UI elements and metadata), validate (compare against baseline), and noop (dry run without output), (5) Context and subcontext concepts for checkpoint management, (6) Screenshot-only mode for full-page captures without DOM scraping, (7) Multiple practical examples covering different scenarios like scraping, validation, multi-language testing, printer profile selection, and platform targeting, (8) Output directory structure documentation, (9) Notes about Excel outputs, screenshot naming conventions, and OSS simulation support. The --target parameter supports multiple platforms: web (default), wingotham, macgotham, android, ios, hpxwindows, hpxmac, hpxandroid, hpxios. The file uses clean CSS styling with code blocks, pre-formatted sections, and color-coded notes. This documentation is essential for understanding how to use the AURA framework from the command line. No code analysis is applicable as this is pure documentation.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module providing utility methods for subscription cancellation flow testing. Contains static methods for selecting cancellation feedback options, verifying cancellation UI elements, validating cancellation timeline, handling plan changes during cancellation, and testing various cancellation retention features like pause plan, transfer subscription, and keep subscription options.

imports.import_statement: from datetime import datetime, time, timedelta

classes.class_purpose: Static helper class providing utility methods for testing subscription cancellation flows. Contains methods for interacting with cancellation feedback forms, verifying cancellation timeline UI, validating retention options, handling plan changes during cancellation, and testing various cancellation-related features. All methods are static and operate on page objects passed as parameters.
classes.how_to_instantiate: CancellationPlanHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call CancellationPlanHelper.select_cancellation_feedback_option(page, 'I do not print enough') directly.

functions.method_signature: def select_cancellation_feedback_option(page, feedback_option="I do not print enough", submit: bool = True)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: feedback_option
parameters.description: Feedback reason to select. Options: 'I do not print enough', 'The service is too expensive', 'I have not received my shipment', 'I have replaced my printer', 'Wi-Fi issues with my printer', 'Other'. Defaults to 'I do not print enough'.

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: Whether to submit the feedback or return to account. Defaults to True.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a cancellation feedback option from the ink subscription cancellation feedback form and optionally submits it. Creates CancellationPage instance. Clicks the continue button with 120 second timeout to proceed to feedback form. Defines option_map dictionary mapping feedback option strings to their corresponding radio button element selectors. Looks up the selector for the provided feedback_option in the map, raising ValueError if not found. Clicks the radio button using page.click() with force=True. If feedback_option is 'Other', fills the feedback text box with 'Other Reason'. If submit parameter is True, clicks the submit feedback button; otherwise clicks the return to account button. This allows tests to select different cancellation reasons and control whether to submit or cancel.

functions.method_signature: def select_paper_cancellation_feedback_option(page, feedback_option="I'm worried I'm going to run out of paper", submit: bool = True)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: feedback_option
parameters.default_value: "I'm worried I'm going to run out of paper"
parameters.description: Paper cancellation feedback reason. Multiple options available including replenishment anxiety, leakage, didn't print enough, ran out, overages, temporary, stockpiling, expensive, damaged, didn't receive, moved, quality, other.

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: Whether to submit or return to account. Defaults to True.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a cancellation feedback option specifically for paper subscription cancellation. Creates CancellationPage instance and clicks continue button with 120 second timeout. Defines comprehensive option_map with 13 paper-specific cancellation reasons mapped to their radio button selectors including replenishment anxiety, leakage, didn't print enough, ran out of paper, paper overages, temporary cancellation, stockpiling, too expensive, damaged paper, didn't receive shipment, moved to non-service area, paper quality issues, and other. Looks up selector for feedback_option, raising ValueError with descriptive message if not found. Clicks the radio button with force=True. If 'Other' is selected, fills feedback text box with 'Other Reason'. If submit is True, clicks submit button; otherwise clicks return to account button. This handles the paper-specific cancellation flow which has different feedback options than ink cancellation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: section_title
parameters.description: Title of the retention section to verify. Options: 'Have your printing needs changed?', 'Pause your subscription for up to 6 months', 'Did you get a new printer?', 'Have questions or need help?', 'Only pay when you need to print'

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific retention option section is displayed correctly on the cancellation page with image, text, and link. Creates CancellationPage instance. Defines link_selectors dictionary mapping section titles to their corresponding link element selectors. Looks up selector for section_title, raising ValueError if not found. Locates the retention option container by finding the link's parent's parent element using XPath navigation. Within the container, locates the image, text paragraph (last p element), and button/link. Uses Playwright expect() to verify the image is visible, the text is visible, the text content matches the section_title, and the link is visible. This ensures retention options are properly displayed to encourage users not to cancel.
functions.usage_example: CancellationPlanHelper.verify_bottom_section(page, 'Pause your subscription for up to 6 months')

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the cancellation timeline page displays all required elements after cancellation is submitted. Creates CancellationTimelinePage instance. Uses Playwright expect() with 90 second timeout to verify header_icon is visible. Verifies header_title, header_subtitle, restore_subscription link, and whats_happens_next section are all visible. The timeline_img verification is commented out. This ensures the cancellation confirmation page loaded correctly with all key elements present.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the cancellation timeline steps display correct dates and content. Creates CancellationTimelinePage instance. Gets today's date and formats as 'MMM DD, YYYY'. Extracts the last day date from step 1 text using regex to find date pattern. Parses the last day date and calculates final bill date as last_day + 1 day. Verifies first step icon and text are visible and contain current date with 'Cancellation submitted'. Verifies second step icon and text are visible and contain last day date with 'Last day to print with Instant Ink cartridges'. For third step, uses regex pattern to match date range format 'MMM DD-DD, YYYY' for cartridge deactivation date, allowing for date range flexibility. Verifies fourth and fifth step icons and text are visible. This ensures the timeline accurately reflects the cancellation schedule with proper date calculations.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the 'Change Your Mind' retention section displays keep subscription and transfer subscription options correctly. Creates CancellationTimelinePage instance. For keep subscription section: verifies keep_subscription_as_it_was element is visible with 90 second timeout, locates the retention option text and link using specific XPath selectors, verifies text is visible with 90 second timeout, asserts text content is 'Restore subscription?' after stripping whitespace, verifies link is visible with 90 second timeout. For transfer subscription section: locates text and link using XPath selectors, verifies text is visible with 90 second timeout, asserts text content is 'Did you get a new printer?' after stripping whitespace, verifies link is visible with 90 second timeout. This ensures both retention options are properly displayed with correct text and clickable links.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: option
parameters.description: Button option to validate. Options: 'Shop HP Ink', 'Preview Upcoming Bill', 'Request Free Recycling Materials', 'Close'

functions.what_it_does: Validates that specific buttons on the cancellation timeline page work correctly by clicking them and verifying expected behavior. Creates CancellationTimelinePage instance. If option is 'Shop HP Ink': verifies shop_hp_button is visible, clicks it expecting a popup, waits for new page, verifies URL contains '/shop/cat', closes new page. If option is 'Preview Upcoming Bill': verifies preview_upcoming_button is visible, clicks expecting popup, waits for new page, closes it. If option is 'Request Free Recycling Materials': verifies request_recycling_button is visible, clicks expecting popup, waits for new page, verifies URL contains '/uat-inktoner-recycle', closes new page. If option is 'Close': verifies close button is visible, clicks it, verifies it becomes not visible. This tests the functionality of action buttons on the cancellation timeline.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the initial state of the cancellation feedback form with all elements in their default state. Creates CancellationPage instance. Verifies cancellation_feedback_title and cancellation_feedback_subtitle are visible. Asserts that all six radio buttons (do not print enough, wifi issues, too expensive, didn't receive shipment, replaced printer, other) are not checked using page.is_checked(). Verifies feedback_text_box is visible and has disabled attribute set (not None). Verifies submit_feedback_button is visible and has disabled attribute set. Verifies return_to_account_button is visible and does not have disabled attribute (is None). This ensures the form loads in the correct initial state with no options selected and submit button disabled until a selection is made.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that cancellation feedback radio buttons are mutually exclusive - only one can be selected at a time. Creates CancellationPage instance. Defines radio_selectors list with all six radio button element selectors. Iterates through each radio button by index. For each radio: locates the parent element using '..' XPath and clicks it to select the radio. Asserts the radio is checked. Then iterates through all other radios and asserts they are not checked. This ensures proper radio button group behavior where selecting one automatically deselects others.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that paper cancellation feedback radio buttons are mutually exclusive with 13 paper-specific options. Creates CancellationPage instance. Defines paper_radio_selectors list with all 13 paper cancellation radio button selectors including replenishment anxiety, leakage, didn't print enough, ran out, overages, temporary, stockpiling, expensive, damaged, didn't receive, moved, quality, and other. Iterates through each radio by index. For each radio: locates parent element and clicks to select. Asserts the radio is checked. Iterates through all other radios and asserts they are not checked. This ensures proper radio button group behavior for the paper-specific cancellation feedback form.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether the button should be enabled. Defaults to False.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the submit feedback button is visible and checks its enabled/disabled state. Creates CancellationPage instance. Verifies submit_feedback_button is visible. If enabled parameter is True, asserts the disabled attribute is None (button is enabled). If enabled is False, asserts the disabled attribute is not None (button is disabled). This allows tests to verify the button state changes appropriately based on form completion.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the cancellation loading page is displayed for at least 4 seconds before transitioning to the timeline page. Creates CancellationPage instance. Records start_time using datetime.now() and prints it. Waits for and verifies printer_img, printer_info_name, and printer_info_serial are visible with 30 second timeout. Verifies cancellation_animation is visible with 30 second timeout. Waits for continue_button to become visible with 90 second timeout indicating timeline page loaded. Records end_time using datetime.now() and prints it. Calculates duration in seconds by subtracting start_time from end_time. Prints the duration. Asserts duration is >= 4 seconds with descriptive error message. Waits for continue_button again. This ensures the loading animation displays long enough for users to see it.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a comprehensive helper module focused entirely on subscription cancellation flow testing. All 38 methods are static, making this a utility class that doesn't require instantiation. Key functional areas: (1) Feedback selection for both ink and paper cancellations with different option sets, (2) Timeline verification with date calculations and step validation, (3) Retention option verification including pause plan, change plan, transfer subscription, and contact support, (4) Radio button behavior testing ensuring mutual exclusivity, (5) Button and link validation with popup handling, (6) Loading page timing verification, (7) Plan change during cancellation with modal interactions. The module heavily uses Playwright's expect() assertions for visibility and content verification. Date handling is sophisticated with regex extraction, parsing, and timedelta calculations to verify timeline dates are correct. The verify_the_steps_on_cancellation_timeline_page method uses flexible date range matching to handle date variations. The module imports 10 different page objects, indicating it orchestrates complex multi-page cancellation flows. No locators are defined in this helper as it delegates to page objects. The static method pattern makes this easily reusable across different test scenarios.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive helper module for dashboard-related test operations including dashboard access, navigation, plan management, billing operations, printer selection, payment method handling (credit card, PayPal, direct debit, Google Pay, Apple Pay), address management, validation of dashboard elements, and various dashboard feature testing. Serves as the central helper for all dashboard and account management test scenarios.

classes.class_purpose: Static helper class providing comprehensive utility methods for dashboard and account management testing. Contains methods for dashboard access and navigation, plan verification and management, billing and payment operations supporting multiple payment gateways (PGS, 2CO) and methods (credit card, PayPal, direct debit, Google Pay, Apple Pay), address management, printer selection, savings calculator validation, session handling, and various dashboard feature testing. All methods are static and operate on page objects passed as parameters.
classes.how_to_instantiate: DashboardHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call DashboardHelper.access(page, 'user@test.com') directly.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address for sign-in if login is required. Required if page redirects to login.

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the dashboard portal and handles authentication if needed. Waits for page load state with 15 second timeout. Navigates to common._portalshell_url with 45 second timeout. Waits for load state again. Attempts to wait for networkidle state, catching and ignoring exceptions if it times out. Checks if 'login' is in the current page URL. If login page is detected and tenant_email is not provided, raises ValueError indicating email is required. If tenant_email is provided, calls HPIDHelper.sign_in() to authenticate. Creates DashboardSideMenuPage instance and gets the visible Instant Ink menu link element. Asserts the element is visible with descriptive error message. This is the primary entry point for accessing the dashboard in tests.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address for authentication

functions.what_it_does: Handles first-time dashboard access by navigating, authenticating, accepting privacy banner, and dismissing all first-time user modals. Calls DashboardHelper.access() with tenant_email to navigate and authenticate. Creates OverviewPage instance. Calls DashboardHelper.accept_banner_and_access_overview_page() to handle privacy banner and navigate to overview. Defines elements list containing lambda functions that return modal elements: special_savings_modal_close, flip_modal_not_now_button, continue_setting_preferences, accept_all_preferences, no_paper_offer_modal, skip_tour, and special_savings_modal_close again. If stack is 'pie' or 'stage', appends another special_savings_modal_close to the list. Performs up to 6 attempts to dismiss modals. In each attempt, iterates through remaining elements, tries to get each element and check if visible, clicks if visible and removes from list, catches exceptions silently. Sleeps 2 seconds between attempts. This ensures a clean dashboard state by dismissing all first-time user experience modals.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: payment_method
parameters.description: Payment method identifier. If None, uses GlobalState.payment_method. Supports credit cards, PayPal, Google Pay, Apple Pay, and direct debit.

functions.what_it_does: Routes to appropriate payment method handler based on payment_method parameter or GlobalState. If payment_method is None, gets it from GlobalState.payment_method. Checks if payment_method contains 'paypal' and calls add_paypal_billing() if true, then returns. Checks if payment_method equals 'google_pay' and calls add_google_pay_billing() if true, then returns. Checks if payment_method equals 'apple_pay' and calls add_apple_pay_billing() if true, then returns. Checks if payment_method contains 'direct_debit' and calls add_direct_debit_billing() if true, then returns. If none of the above, gets card_payment_gateway from GlobalState. If gateway is 'PGS', calls add_pgs_billing(). If gateway is '2CO', calls add_2co_billing(). Otherwise raises ValueError with unsupported payment provider message. This is the main entry point for adding any payment method, routing to specialized handlers.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: card_type
parameters.description: Type of credit card to use. If None, uses default from payment data.

functions.what_it_does: Adds credit card payment method through PGS (Payment Gateway Service) with 3DS authentication support. Creates ConfirmationPage and ShippingBillingPage instances. Gets payment data using common.get_payment_method_data() with card_type. Logs the card type being used. If country code is 'IT', fills tax_id_input with Italian tax ID. Checks if billing_continue_button is enabled and clicks it. Waits for PGS iframe to be visible with 120 second timeout. Gets frame locator for the iframe. Fills card number input with credit card number from payment data. Selects expiration month and year from dropdowns. Types CVV into cvv_input. Checks if billing_next_button exists in frame and clicks it if present. Otherwise clicks sca_save_button for Strong Customer Authentication. Handles 3DS challenge: waits for either redirectTo3ds1Frame or iframe_2fa to appear with 5 second timeout, gets the appropriate challenge frame locator, waits for authentication_result_2fa dropdown, selects authentication result from payment data (defaults to 'AUTHENTICATED'), waits for and clicks the submit button. Waits for update_shipping_billing_message to appear with 60 second timeout confirming success. This handles the complete PGS payment flow including 3DS2 authentication.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: card_type
parameters.description: Type of credit card to use

functions.docstring: Handle 2Checkout payment method for billing page
functions.what_it_does: Adds credit card payment method through 2Checkout payment gateway. Creates ShippingBillingPage, ConfirmationPage instances. Gets payment data for card_type. Logs card type. Checks billing_continue_button is enabled and clicks it. Waits for 2CO iframe to be visible with 120 second timeout. Waits for and clicks continue_to_billing button in iframe with 60 second timeout.
